questions.question: CI/CD for ML differs from software CI/CD because:
options: It must also validate data quality and model performance -- not just code correctness
questions.explanation: Software passes if unit tests pass. ML must also pass: data schema, performance threshold, no regression.

questions.question: A model quality gate blocks deployment when:
options: Validation AUC falls below a threshold, exceeds inference latency, or regresses vs. production
questions.explanation: Objective thresholds prevent deploying degraded models automatically.

questions.question: Data validation in ML CI/CD checks for:
options: Schema changes, distribution drift, missing values, and range violations
questions.explanation: Great Expectations, Pandera validate data before training begins.

questions.question: Blue-green deployment for ML:
options: Runs old model (blue) and new model (green) simultaneously, routes fraction of traffic to green to test
questions.explanation: Allows instant rollback by switching traffic back to blue if green underperforms.

questions.question: A canary release routes:
options: A small percentage (5%) of traffic to the new model before full rollout
questions.explanation: Monitor metrics on 5% traffic. If good, gradually increase to 100%.

questions.question: model.pkl in a Git repo is problematic because:
options: Binary files cause large repos, can't diff, and expose model weights -- use S3/HuggingFace Hub instead
questions.explanation: Model files can be hundreds of MB. Store in model registries (MLflow, HuggingFace Hub, S3).

questions.question: Shadow mode testing for ML models:
options: Runs new model in parallel with production, logging predictions without serving them -- validate before cutover
questions.explanation: Compare new model's outputs to production on real traffic with zero user impact.

questions.question: Feature drift detection monitors:
options: Changes in the statistical distribution of input features between training time and serving time
questions.explanation: If customers start sending different data than the model was trained on, performance degrades.

questions.question: The recommended trigger for automated model retraining:
options: When performance metrics drop below threshold OR on a scheduled cadence
questions.explanation: Performance-triggered retraining responds to actual degradation. Schedule ensures freshness.

questions.question: The difference between model retraining and model deployment CI/CD:
options: Retraining CI/CD: triggered by data drift or schedule. Deployment CI/CD: triggered by code/config changes.
questions.explanation: Both pipelines exist in mature MLOps. Data-triggered retraining is separate from code-triggered deployment.
